skill.name: aws-cost-cleanup
skill.description: Automated cleanup of unused AWS resources to reduce costs
skill.summary: Automated tool to identify and remove unused AWS resources like unattached EBS volumes, old snapshots, and unused Elastic IPs to reduce cloud costs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill uses legitimate AWS CLI commands for cost optimization. Network calls are to official AWS documentation URLs only. AWS service names flagged as C2 keywords are standard terminology. This is a valid DevOps tool for AWS cost cleanup.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: AWS CLI commands used for legitimate resource cleanup operations. Commands are hardcoded strings calling AWS APIs, not user input.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Documentation Links
dangerous_patterns.description: Hardcoded URLs to official AWS documentation sites

dangerous_patterns.title: AWS Service Names Flagged as C2 Keywords
dangerous_patterns.description: Standard AWS terminology (EC2, EBS, Lambda) and DevOps terms (cleanup, delete, remove) incorrectly flagged as C2 indicators

dangerous_patterns.title: AWS Resource Discovery Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Standard AWS describe commands for resource discovery - legitimate DevOps practice

content.value_statement: Automate identification and removal of unused AWS resources to eliminate waste and reduce monthly cloud bills.
actual_capabilities: Identify unattached EBS volumes and calculate potential savings
Detect and clean up old EBS snapshots older than 90 days
Find and release unused Elastic IP addresses
Create S3 lifecycle policies for cost optimization
Generate cost impact reports for cleanup operations
Set up automated Lambda functions for scheduled cleanup
limitations: Does not delete resources that are actively in use
Requires AWS credentials with appropriate IAM permissions
Cannot recover accidentally deleted resources without backups
May require manual review for resources with dependencies

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Cost Optimization
use_cases.description: Automate weekly cleanup of unused resources across multiple AWS accounts to reduce monthly cloud spending.

use_cases.title: Cloud FinOps Teams
use_cases.description: Generate reports on unused resources and potential savings to inform budget decisions and cost allocation.
use_cases.target_user: Finance and operations teams tracking cloud costs

use_cases.title: Startup Cloud Management
use_cases.description: Quickly identify and remove test and dev resources that are no longer needed to keep cloud bills manageable.
use_cases.target_user: Small teams and startups with limited cloud expertise

prompt_templates.title: Find unused AWS resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to find all unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, and old snapshots in my AWS account. Generate a report showing potential monthly savings.

prompt_templates.title: Create cleanup script
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to create a bash script that identifies unattached EBS volumes older than 30 days and calculates the total size and cost impact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a cleanup script for review

prompt_templates.title: Set up automated cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to create a Lambda function that runs weekly to identify and delete unattached EBS volumes older than 7 days. Include dry-run mode.

prompt_templates.title: Cost impact analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to calculate the monthly and annual cost savings if we clean up all unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, and snapshots older than 90 days.

output_examples.input: Find all unused resources and calculate potential savings
output_examples.output:
Unattached EBS Volumes: 5
- vol-0abc123 (100 GB) - $10.00/month
- vol-0def456 (50 GB) - $5.00/month
- vol-0ghi789 (200 GB) - $20.00/month

Unused Elastic IPs: 3 - $10.95/month
Old Snapshots: 12 - $15.00/month

Total Monthly Savings: $70.95
Annual Savings: $851.40

output_examples.input: Create a cleanup script for unattached EBS volumes
output_examples.output:
#!/bin/bash
# cleanup-unused-ebs.sh - Dry-run mode

echo "Finding unattached EBS volumes..."
VOLUMES=$(aws ec2 describe-volumes \
  --filters Name=status,Values=available \
  --query 'Volumes[*].VolumeId' \
  --output text)

for vol in $VOLUMES; do
  echo "Would delete: $vol"
done

echo "Review and remove 'Would delete' lines to enable actual deletion"

Lambda function created: weekly-ebs-cleanup
- Runtime: Python 3.9
- Trigger: CloudWatch Events (every Sunday 2 AM)
- Retention: Deletes volumes older than 7 days
- Dry-run: Enabled by default
- SNS notification: Enabled for deletion alerts

best_practices: Always run cleanup scripts in dry-run mode first to review what will be deleted
Use resource tags to identify owners before deletion and notify stakeholders
Create final snapshots of critical data before deletion as a safety net
anti_patterns: Deleting resources without reviewing the cleanup list first
Ignoring resource dependencies - check for attached ENIs before deleting volumes
Skipping the safety checklist - always verify resources are truly unused

faq.question: Will this accidentally delete resources I still need?
faq.answer: The skill includes dry-run mode by default. All deletion commands are commented out until you explicitly enable them. Always review the output before removing comments.

faq.question: What AWS permissions are required?
faq.answer: You need read access (describe-*) for discovery and write access (delete-*) for cleanup. Use IAM roles with least privilege.

faq.question: Can this run across multiple AWS accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports AWS Organizations integration. You can iterate across all accounts in your organization.

faq.question: How do I recover accidentally deleted resources?
faq.answer: Create snapshots before deletion. Without snapshots, deleted resources cannot be recovered. Enable AWS Backup for critical data.

faq.question: What is the recommended cleanup schedule?
faq.answer: Weekly discovery with monthly cleanup is recommended. This gives teams time to identify and claim resources they still need.

faq.question: Does this work with AWS Backup?
faq.answer: The skill complements AWS Backup by cleaning up resources that are not protected by backup policies. Use both together for complete coverage.
